included_relationships.notes: Leave is triggered by the employee's own qualifying life event (birth, adoption placement, or foster care placement of the employee's child). No covered-person relationship beyond the employee is defined in the regulation.

required_value.unit: months immediately preceding first requested leave date

conditions.notes: Rehired ERS retirees receiving annuity payments are ineligible during the first 1,040 hours of work in the calendar year, whether salaried or hourly.
conditions.source_text: birth of the employee's child; if salaried, the employee must have six continuous months of employment; if hourly, the employee must have worked 700 hours in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.

required_value.required_value: placement of a minor child for adoption with the employee

required_value.unit: months immediately preceding first requested leave date

conditions.notes: Rehired ERS retirees receiving annuity payments are ineligible during the first 1,040 hours of work in the calendar year, whether salaried or hourly.
conditions.source_text: placement of a minor child for adoption with the employee; if salaried, the employee must have six continuous months of employment; if hourly, the employee must have worked 700 hours in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.

required_value.required_value: placement of a minor child for foster care with the employee

required_value.unit: months immediately preceding first requested leave date

conditions.notes: Rehired ERS retirees receiving annuity payments are ineligible during the first 1,040 hours of work in the calendar year, whether salaried or hourly.
conditions.source_text: placement of a minor child for foster care with the employee; if salaried, the employee must have six continuous months of employment; if hourly, the employee must have worked 700 hours in the six months immediately preceding the first requested paid parental leave date.

relationship_issues: included_relationships includes 'child' as a relationship, but the regulation text does not describe leave taken to care for a child — it describes leave triggered by the birth of the employee's own child. The relationship is 'self' (the employee's own qualifying life event). 'child' as a covered-person relationship is not supported by the regulation chunks.
logic_issues: The unit for the hourly hours check reads 'hours in preceding 6 months' — the time window detail belongs in a separate nested check or notes, not in unit. Unit should be measurement-only (e.g., 'hours').
The employment type and tenure sub-check uses 'employment type and tenure' as a field label, which is vague. It should be split or labeled more specifically.
missing_conditions: The regulation lists three qualifying life events: (i) birth of employee's child, (ii) placement of a minor child for adoption, (iii) placement of a minor child for foster care. API1 only captures birth. The other two qualifying events are missing as parallel top-level conditions.
unsupported_conditions: 'child' in included_relationships is not supported as a covered-person relationship by the regulation chunks.
format_issues: unit for hourly check contains a time window phrase ('hours in preceding 6 months') rather than a measurement-only unit ('hours').
revision_summary: Removed 'child' from included_relationships; only 'self' is supported by the regulation.
Added two missing parallel top-level conditions for adoption placement and foster care placement.
Fixed unit for hourly hours check to 'hours' only; moved the 6-month window into a separate nested check.
Kept the rehired ERS retiree ineligibility note on each condition as it applies to all three qualifying events.
Kept salaried and hourly tenure sub-checks nested inside each qualifying event condition.
